name: Testing Workflow with Coverage Reports
description: Run Go tests with race detection, generate coverage reports, and visualize coverage in the browser.
trendingReason: Coverage-driven development is now standard in Go CI pipelines

steps.title: Run all tests with verbose output
steps.description: Execute the full test suite with verbose output and race detection enabled.
steps.tip: The -race flag detects data races at runtime. Always use it in CI.

steps.title: Generate a coverage profile
steps.description: Run tests and output a coverage profile file for analysis.

steps.title: View coverage summary by function
steps.description: Display a breakdown of coverage percentage per function.

steps.title: Open coverage report in the browser
steps.description: Generate an HTML coverage report and open it for visual inspection.
steps.tip: Green lines are covered, red lines are not. Focus on uncovered branches in critical paths.

steps.title: Enforce minimum coverage threshold
steps.description: Parse coverage output and fail the build if total coverage is below a threshold.
steps.command: go test ./... -coverprofile=coverage.out && total=$(go tool cover -func=coverage.out | grep total | awk '{print $3}' | tr -d '%') && echo "Coverage: ${total}%" && [ $(echo "$total >= 70" | bc) -eq 1 ] || (echo 'FAIL: coverage below 70%' && exit 1)
steps.warning: Set a realistic threshold. Forcing 100% coverage leads to low-value tests.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Go Test Coverage Workflow
set -euo pipefail

THRESHOLD=${1:-70}

echo "=== Running Tests with Race Detection ==="
go test ./... -v -race -coverprofile=coverage.out -covermode=atomic

echo ""
echo "=== Coverage by Function ==="
go tool cover -func=coverage.out

echo ""
echo "=== Coverage Summary ==="
TOTAL=$(go tool cover -func=coverage.out | grep total | awk '{print $3}' | tr -d '%')
echo "Total coverage: ${TOTAL}%"
echo "Threshold: ${THRESHOLD}%"

if [ "$(echo "$TOTAL >= $THRESHOLD" | bc)" -eq 1 ]; then
  echo "PASS: Coverage meets threshold"
else
  echo "FAIL: Coverage below threshold"
  exit 1
fi

echo ""
echo "=== Generate HTML Report ==="
go tool cover -html=coverage.out -o coverage.html
echo "Open coverage.html in your browser to view the report"

faq.question: What is the difference between covermode set and atomic?
faq.answer: The 'set' mode records whether a statement was executed. The 'atomic' mode counts exact executions and is safe with -race. Use 'atomic' in CI for accuracy.

faq.question: How do I exclude generated files from coverage?
faq.answer: Add '//go:build !coverage' build tags to generated files, or filter them in your CI script by grepping the coverage output.

faq.question: Can I merge coverage from multiple test runs?
faq.answer: Yes. Use 'go test -coverprofile' for each package separately, then merge with tools like gocovmerge or the built-in -coverpkg flag to measure cross-package coverage.
